Step 5: File a consumer complaint if money was paid for a worthless degree
authority: UGC → AICTE → AIU (for foreign degrees) → State Higher Education Department → Consumer Forum

scenario: Internet service not working, broadband connection disconnected without reason, ISP overcharging, poor internet speed not matching plan, mobile data issues, telecom service complaint.
title: Internet / Telecom Service Complaint
steps: Step 1: Register a complaint with the ISP or telecom operator's customer care
Step 2: If unresolved, file a complaint on the TRAI portal (trai.gov.in) or call 1800-110-420
Step 3: Approach the Telecom Disputes Settlement and Appellate Tribunal (TDSAT)
Step 4: File a consumer complaint for deficiency of service
documents: Internet/telecom subscription details and plan documents
Speed test results or screenshots of connectivity issues
provisions: Telecom Regulatory Authority of India Act 1997
authority: ISP/Telecom Operator → TRAI Helpline (1800-110-420) → TDSAT → Consumer Forum

scenario: Neighbours dispute about boundary wall, common wall repair disagreement, shared terrace conflict, parking space dispute in apartment, apartment committee fraud, maintenance not collected properly.
title: Housing Society / Apartment Dispute
steps: Step 1: Send a written complaint to the Housing Society Committee
Step 2: Attend the General Body Meeting and raise the issue formally
Step 3: File a complaint with the Registrar of Cooperative Societies
Step 4: For RERA-registered societies, file on the state RERA portal
Step 5: Approach Consumer Forum for deficiency in services
documents: Meeting minutes where issue was raised
provisions: Maharashtra Apartment Ownership Act (and similar state acts)
authority: Housing Society Committee → Registrar of Cooperative Societies → RERA → Consumer Forum

scenario: Private hospital overcharging for treatment, excessive medical bill, hospital charging more than declared rates, hidden charges in medical bill, insurance cashless denied by hospital.
title: Hospital Overcharging / Medical Bill Dispute
steps: Step 1: Request detailed itemised bill from the hospital
Step 2: Compare charges with government-declared package rates (if CGHS/PMJAY empanelled)
Step 3: File a complaint with the hospital's billing department and grievance officer
Step 4: File a complaint with the State Medical Council
Step 5: File a consumer complaint for overcharging and unfair trade practice
Step 6: For insurance cashless denial, approach the Insurance Ombudsman
authority: Hospital Grievance Officer → State Medical Council → Consumer Forum → Insurance Ombudsman

scenario: Surrogate mother rights violated, surrogacy agreement broken, intended parents abandoning child, commercial surrogacy dispute, IVF clinic fraud, ART clinic complaint.
title: Surrogacy / ART Clinic Dispute
steps: Step 1: Approach the National Board for ART and Surrogacy for complaints
Step 2: File a complaint with the state ART Authority
Step 3: Consult a family lawyer immediately as surrogacy agreements are legally complex
Step 4: File a civil suit if surrogacy agreement is breached
Step 5: Approach the High Court for protection of the child's rights
documents: Medical records and IVF treatment documents
Aadhaar Card and ID proof of all parties
provisions: Guardian and Wards Act 1890 (for child custody)
authority: State ART Authority → National Board for ART and Surrogacy → Family Court → High Court

scenario: Travel agent fraud, tour package money taken but trip not arranged, fake visa promise, travel agency closed after taking money, hotel booking fraud, flight cancellation refund not given.
title: Travel Agent / Tour Package Fraud
steps: Step 1: File an FIR at the police station for cheating and fraud
Step 2: File a complaint with the Ministry of Tourism against the travel agency
Step 3: File a consumer complaint for deficiency of service
Step 4: Contact IATA (International Air Transport Association) if airline ticket fraud
Step 5: Approach your credit card company for chargeback if payment was by card
documents: Tour package agreement or booking confirmation
provisions: Foreign Exchange Management Act 1999 (for foreign tour related forex issues)
authority: Police Station → Ministry of Tourism → Consumer Forum → Credit Card Chargeback

scenario: Matrimonial property dispute after divorce, husband hiding assets during divorce, wife not getting share of property, streedhan not returned, gold jewellery not given back after separation.
title: Matrimonial Property / Streedhan Dispute
steps: Step 1: Consult a family lawyer immediately to file for matrimonial property rights
Step 2: File a petition in Family Court for return of streedhan and matrimonial property
Step 3: File a police complaint for Section 406 IPC if streedhan is criminally withheld
Step 4: Apply for interim attachment of property if husband may dispose of assets
Step 5: Approach High Court if Family Court delays
documents: List and proof of streedhan (jewellery receipts, photographs)
provisions: Section 406 IPC (Criminal breach of trust — for streedhan)
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005
authority: Police Station (for streedhan) → Family Court → High Court

scenario: Public sector bank merger affecting FD or account, cooperative bank failed and deposits stuck, PMJDY account issue, zero balance account closed without notice, bank account frozen without reason.
title: Bank Account / Deposit Dispute
steps: Step 1: File a written complaint with the bank branch manager
Step 2: Escalate to the bank's nodal grievance officer
Step 3: If unresolved in 30 days, approach the Banking Ombudsman
Step 4: For cooperative bank failure, approach the State Cooperative Department
Step 5: File a complaint on RBI's Sachet portal or CGPDM portal
provisions: Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation Act 1961 (DICGC — insurance up to Rs 5 lakh)
authority: Bank Branch → Banking Ombudsman → RBI → State Cooperative Dept (for cooperative banks) → Consumer Forum

scenario: Social media platform blocking my account, YouTube channel deleted wrongly, content removed unfairly, platform shadowbanning, account suspended without reason, appeal not resolved.
title: Social Media Platform / Content Moderation Dispute
steps: Step 1: Use the platform's internal appeals process for account restoration
Step 2: File a complaint with the platform's Grievance Officer as per IT Rules 2021
Step 3: Escalate to the Appellate Committee under IT Rules 2021 if grievance not resolved
Step 4: Approach the Ministry of Electronics and IT (MeitY) for unresolved platform issues
Step 5: File a civil suit for wrongful content removal causing financial damage
documents: Screenshot of removed content or suspended account
Your appeals submitted to the platform
provisions: IT (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules 2021
authority: Platform Grievance Officer → IT Rules Appellate Committee → MeitY → High Court

scenario: Illegal surveillance, someone installed spy camera in my room or bathroom, peeping tom, neighbour recording me without consent, private videos leaked, CCTV pointed into private area.
title: Illegal Surveillance / Privacy Violation
steps: Step 1: Do not disturb the camera — photograph its location as evidence
Step 2: File an FIR at the police station immediately
Step 3: File a complaint on cybercrime.gov.in
Step 4: If videos are circulating online, report to the platform and cyber crime cell
Step 5: Approach court for injunction to prevent further spread of content
provisions: Section 66E IT Act (Violation of privacy — capturing private images)
Section 67A IT Act (Publishing sexually explicit material)
authority: Police Station → Cyber Crime Cell → Court (for injunction) → Platform (for content removal)

scenario: Government employee discrimination, promotion denied due to caste or religion, transfer as punishment, seniority ignored, government service dispute, departmental enquiry wrongly initiated.
title: Government Service / Employment Discrimination
steps: Step 1: File a representation to the Head of Department stating grievance
Step 2: Approach the Central Administrative Tribunal (CAT) for central government employees
Step 3: For state government employees, approach the State Administrative Tribunal or High Court
Step 4: File a complaint with the SC/ST Commission if caste discrimination is involved
Step 5: File a writ petition in High Court for violation of service rules
provisions: Article 16 of Constitution (equality in public employment)
SC/ST Prevention of Atrocities Act (if caste discrimination)
authority: Head of Department → Central Administrative Tribunal (CAT) → High Court → Supreme Court

scenario: Court order not being followed, decree holder not getting money despite court order, contempt of court by other party, injunction violated, execution of decree delayed.
title: Court Order Violation / Contempt of Court
steps: Step 1: File an Execution Petition in the court that passed the order
Step 2: File a Contempt of Court application against the person violating the order
Step 3: The court can impose fines or imprisonment for contempt
Step 4: For execution of money decree, attach the property or bank accounts of the defaulter
Step 5: File a fresh application for civil imprisonment if all else fails
documents: Proof that the order is being violated
provisions: Section 56 CPC (Arrest and imprisonment in execution)
authority: Same Court that passed the order → High Court (contempt) → Supreme Court

scenario: Notary fraud, stamp paper fraud, fake affidavit, document attestation fraud, self-attested document misused, power of attorney misused.
title: Document Fraud / Notary Fraud
steps: Step 1: File an FIR at the police station for forgery and fraud
Step 2: File a complaint with the District Magistrate who supervises notaries
Step 3: Report to the Bar Council of India if an advocate is involved
Step 4: File a civil suit to declare the fraudulent document void
Step 5: Revoke any power of attorney that is being misused through a registered deed
authority: Police Station → District Magistrate → Bar Council (if advocate involved) → Civil Court

scenario: Panchayat not doing work, sarpanch misusing funds, gram panchayat fund embezzlement, village development work not done, muster roll fraud, NREGA site officer corruption.
title: Panchayat / Local Body Corruption
steps: Step 1: Raise the issue at the Gram Sabha meeting formally
Step 2: File a complaint with the Block Development Officer (BDO)
Step 3: File a complaint with the District Collector
Step 4: File an RTI to get information on fund utilisation
Step 5: File a complaint with the State Vigilance and Anti-Corruption Bureau
documents: Photographs of incomplete or fake work
authority: Gram Sabha → Block Development Officer → District Collector → State Vigilance Bureau → Lokayukta

scenario: Newspaper or TV channel published false news about me, media defamation, journalist publishing private information, TV channel airing false allegations, magazine spreading rumors about me.
title: Media Defamation / False Publication
steps: Step 1: Send a legal notice to the publication demanding retraction and apology
Step 2: File a complaint with the Press Council of India for print media
Step 3: File a complaint with the News Broadcasting Standards Authority (NBSA) for TV news
Step 4: File a defamation case (civil or criminal) in court
Step 5: Claim damages for reputational harm through civil court
documents: Copy of the defamatory article, broadcast, or publication
Proof that the published content is false
Evidence of reputation damage (loss of business, job etc.)
authority: Press Council of India → NBSA → Police Station (criminal defamation) → Civil Court (damages)

scenario: Trespassing on my land, someone entering my house without permission, breaking into property, squatting on private land, illegal occupation of my commercial space.
title: Trespass / Illegal Occupation of Property
steps: Step 1: File a complaint at the police station for criminal trespass
Step 2: Send a legal notice to the trespasser through a lawyer
Step 3: File a civil suit for injunction and eviction
Step 4: Approach the Revenue Authority (Tahsildar) for land trespass
Step 5: Apply for an urgent stay order from court if property is being damaged
authority: Police Station → Revenue Authority → Civil Court → High Court

scenario: Drunk driving accident, driving under influence of alcohol caused injury, drunk driver hit my vehicle, alcohol related road accident complaint, breathalyzer test demanded.
title: Drunk Driving / DUI Complaint
steps: Step 1: Call police 112 immediately at the accident scene
Step 2: Demand breathalyzer test from traffic police for the drunk driver
Step 3: File an FIR for dangerous driving and causing injury
Step 4: Seek medical treatment and keep all records
Step 5: File MACT claim for compensation for injuries and vehicle damage
provisions: Section 185 Motor Vehicles Act (drunk driving — punishment)
Section 337/338 IPC (causing hurt by dangerous act)
authority: Police (112) → Police Station → MACT (for compensation) → Criminal Court

scenario: Mining royalty dispute, fisherman rights violated, farmer crop damage by flood or drought, crop insurance claim rejected, PM Fasal Bima Yojana complaint, natural disaster compensation not received.
title: Crop Insurance / Agricultural Compensation
steps: Step 1: Notify the insurance company within 72 hours of crop loss
Step 2: File a complaint on the PMFBY portal (pmfby.gov.in)
Step 3: Contact your nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) for claim assistance
Step 4: Approach the Agriculture Department for government compensation
Step 5: File a consumer complaint if insurance company rejects valid claim
documents: PMFBY insurance policy and premium receipt
Loan account details (if crop loan taken)
provisions: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana Guidelines
authority: Insurance Company → Agriculture Department → PMFBY Portal → Consumer Forum

scenario: Neighbour causing problems, neighbour encroaching on common area, noisy neighbour, neighbour damaging my property, civil dispute with person living nearby.
title: Neighbour Dispute
steps: Step 1: Try to resolve the dispute amicably through direct conversation
Step 2: If unsuccessful, send a written notice to the neighbour
Step 3: File a complaint with the local police station for nuisance or trespass
Step 4: File a civil suit for injunction if the neighbour is causing damage
Step 5: Approach the local municipal authority for encroachment on common areas
documents: Photographs or videos of the issue
authority: Police Station → Municipal Authority → Civil Court

scenario: Missing person, child missing, family member not found, person disappeared suddenly, runaway teen, suspicious disappearance, person missing from hospital or custody.
title: Missing Person / Disappearance
steps: Step 1: File a missing person report at the nearest police station immediately — no waiting period required
Step 2: Register on the TrackChild portal (trackthemissingchild.gov.in) for missing children
Step 3: Contact Childline 1098 if a child is missing
Step 4: File a habeas corpus petition in High Court if suspicious circumstances
Step 5: Share details on the National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) missing person portal
documents: Aadhaar Card of missing person and complainant
Contact details of people who last saw the person
Any communication received from missing person
authority: Police Station → Childline 1098 (for children) → TrackChild Portal → High Court (Habeas Corpus)

scenario: Gambling den in neighbourhood, illegal betting operation, online gambling fraud, cricket match fixing, sports betting scam, casino fraud.
title: Illegal Gambling / Betting Fraud
steps: Step 1: File a complaint with the local police — illegal gambling dens can be raided
Step 2: File a complaint on cybercrime.gov.in for online gambling fraud
Step 3: Report to the Economic Offences Wing for large-scale betting operations
Step 4: File an FIR for cheating if money was fraudulently taken
documents: Proof of money lost in gambling fraud
authority: Local Police Station → Economic Offences Wing → Cyber Crime Cell (for online gambling)

scenario: Piracy of my intellectual property, copyright infringement, someone copied my book or software, plagiarism of my work, patent infringement, trademark stolen.
title: Intellectual Property / Copyright Infringement
steps: Step 1: Document all evidence of infringement with screenshots, URLs, and copies
Step 2: Send a cease and desist notice to the infringer through a lawyer
Step 3: File a complaint with the Copyright Office of India
Step 4: For online infringement, send a DMCA takedown to the hosting platform
Step 5: File a civil suit for damages and injunction in Intellectual Property Division of High Court
documents: Proof of original creation (original files with metadata, registration)
authority: Copyright Office → Platform (DMCA/takedown) → Intellectual Property Division of High Court

scenario: Acid attack on face or body, chemical thrown on me, someone threw acid or corrosive substance causing burns and disfigurement.
title: Acid Attack
steps: Step 1: Rush to the nearest hospital immediately — flush affected area with water
Step 2: Hospital must provide free treatment under Supreme Court guidelines
Step 3: File an FIR at the police station immediately
Step 4: Collect Medico-Legal Certificate (MLC) from the hospital
Step 5: Apply for compensation from the State Legal Services Authority
Step 6: Consult a lawyer for maximum compensation under IPC and victim compensation scheme
provisions: Section 326A IPC (Acid attack — minimum 10 years, up to life)
Supreme Court guidelines on free acid attack treatment
authority: Nearest Hospital (free treatment) → Police Station → State Legal Services Authority (compensation)

scenario: Child abuse, minor being abused physically or sexually, child labour, child trafficking, exploitation of children, POCSO case, sexual assault on minor.
title: Child Abuse / POCSO Offence
steps: Step 1: Report immediately to police — POCSO mandates FIR registration
Step 2: Take the child for medical examination at a government hospital
Step 3: Contact the Child Welfare Committee (CWC) for child's protection
Step 4: POCSO court provides special fast-track trial within one year
Step 5: Child's identity must be kept confidential throughout proceedings
Step 6: Apply for victim compensation under POCSO compensation scheme
documents: Aadhaar Card of parent or guardian
provisions: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act 2012
authority: Police Station (POCSO unit) → Child Welfare Committee → Special POCSO Court → NCPCR

scenario: Drunk driving accident, hit and run case, vehicle accident caused by rash driving, road accident injury or death, reckless driving causing harm.
title: Road Accident / Rash Driving
steps: Step 1: Call 108 (ambulance) and get injured persons to hospital immediately
Step 2: File an FIR at the nearest police station with vehicle number of the offender
Step 3: Collect Medico-Legal Certificate and all medical bills
Step 4: File a claim before the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) for compensation
Step 5: Inform your own vehicle insurance company
Step 6: If hit and run, claim compensation from Solatium Fund through MACT
provisions: Section 338 IPC (Grievous hurt by rash act)
Motor Vehicles Act 1988 — Section 161 (Hit and Run compensation)
authority: Police Station → Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) → Insurance Company → Solatium Fund (hit and run)

scenario: Human trafficking, forced prostitution, forced labour, bonded labour, person kidnapped and sold, trafficking of women or children for exploitation.
title: Human Trafficking / Bonded Labour
steps: Step 1: Report immediately to police or Anti-Human Trafficking Unit (AHTU)
Step 2: Call National Human Trafficking Helpline: 1800-419-8588
Step 3: Approach the District Magistrate for rescue and rehabilitation
Step 4: Victim can apply for witness protection during trial
Step 5: Apply for compensation and rehabilitation under victim compensation scheme
Step 6: NGOs like iCall and Shakti Vahini can provide support
documents: Any documents proving bonded labour or trafficking
provisions: Section 370 IPC (Trafficking of persons — up to 10 years)
authority: Anti-Human Trafficking Unit (AHTU) → District Magistrate → Special Court → National Human Trafficking Helpline (1800-419-8588)

scenario: Drug possession, drug trafficking, narcotics case, NDPS case, someone planted drugs on me, false drug case, arrested for drugs.
title: Drug / Narcotics Case (NDPS)
steps: Step 1: Do not make any statement to police without a lawyer present
Step 2: Demand that the search and seizure was done in front of an independent witness (panch)
Step 3: Contact a criminal lawyer immediately for bail application
Step 4: Challenge the seizure procedure if proper NDPS Act protocol was not followed
Step 5: If falsely implicated, gather evidence to prove you were not in possession
Step 6: Apply for bail — for small quantities, bail is possible; commercial quantities require Special Court
documents: Medical examination at time of arrest
provisions: Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act 1985
Section 37 NDPS (Bail conditions for commercial quantity)
Section 50 NDPS (Right to be searched before Gazetted Officer)
authority: Criminal Lawyer (immediately) → Sessions Court / Special NDPS Court → High Court (for bail)

scenario: Rape or sexual assault, sexual violence against woman, molestation, outrage of modesty, unwanted sexual contact, sexual abuse by known or unknown person.
title: Sexual Assault / Rape
steps: Step 1: Go to the nearest government hospital for free medical treatment and forensic examination
Step 2: File an FIR — police cannot refuse; you can also record statement before a Magistrate
Step 3: Your identity must be kept completely confidential
Step 4: A female police officer must record your statement
Step 5: Apply for interim compensation under victim compensation scheme
Step 6: Fast-track court must complete trial within 2 months
documents: Clothes worn at time of assault (preserved as evidence)
provisions: Section 376 IPC (Rape — minimum 10 years, up to life)
authority: Government Hospital (free treatment) → Police Station → Fast Track Court → State Legal Services Authority (compensation)

scenario: Dowry demand, in-laws harassing for more dowry, husband demanding cash or gold, threatened for not bringing dowry, dowry death, bride burning.
title: Dowry Harassment / Dowry Death
steps: Step 1: File an FIR immediately under Section 498A IPC and Dowry Prohibition Act
Step 2: Go to a government hospital if there are physical injuries and get MLC done
Step 3: Contact the Women's Helpline (181) or One Stop Centre for shelter and support
Step 4: Apply for Protection Order under the Domestic Violence Act
Step 5: The husband and in-laws can be arrested for dowry harassment
Step 6: In case of dowry death, the presumption of guilt is on husband's family
documents: Evidence of dowry demands (messages, voice recordings, emails)
Wedding photographs and list of dowry given
provisions: Section 498A IPC (Cruelty for dowry — up to 3 years)
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005
authority: Police Station → Women Helpline (181) → One Stop Centre → Family Court → Fast Track Court

scenario: Murder case, attempt to murder, someone tried to kill me, my family member was killed, homicide, unnatural death, suspicious death.
title: Murder / Attempt to Murder
steps: Step 1: Call 100 (police) and 108 (ambulance) immediately
Step 2: Preserve the crime scene — do not touch or move anything
Step 3: File an FIR immediately with names of suspected persons
Step 4: Insist on post-mortem examination at a government hospital
Step 5: Collect all evidence — CCTV footage, witness details
Step 6: If police are inactive, approach the Judicial Magistrate directly
provisions: Section 302 IPC (Murder — death penalty or life imprisonment)
Section 307 IPC (Attempt to murder — up to life)
Section 304 IPC (Culpable homicide not amounting to murder)
Section 174 CrPC (Police inquest on suspicious death)
authority: Police Station (100) → Judicial Magistrate → Sessions Court → High Court

scenario: Terrorist activity, bomb threat, national security threat, sedition, waging war against the nation, anti-national activities, unlawful assembly planning violence.
title: Terrorism / National Security Threat
steps: Step 1: Report immediately to police (100) or NIA helpline
Step 2: Do not share the information publicly or on social media
Step 3: Preserve all evidence — documents, devices, communications
Step 4: Cooperate fully with investigating agencies
Step 5: If falsely accused, immediately engage a senior criminal lawyer
Step 6: Bail under UAPA is extremely difficult — legal counsel is critical
documents: Any evidence of the threat (messages, devices, documents)
provisions: Section 121 IPC (Waging war against Government of India)
authority: Police (100) → NIA (National Investigation Agency) → Special NIA Court

scenario: Passport fraud, fake visa, immigration fraud, overstaying visa, illegal immigration, forged travel documents, fake foreign degree certificate.
title: Passport / Visa / Immigration Fraud
steps: Step 1: Report forged documents to the nearest police station
Step 2: File a complaint with the Passport Seva Kendra or Regional Passport Office
Step 3: For visa fraud, report to the respective country's embassy and Bureau of Immigration
Step 4: If you are a victim of immigration fraud by an agent, file FIR against the agent
Step 5: Contact FRRO (Foreigners Regional Registration Office) if visa overstay is involved
documents: Payment receipts to agent (if agent fraud)
provisions: Section 468 IPC (Forgery for the purpose of cheating)
Section 471 IPC (Using forged document as genuine)
authority: Police Station → Passport Seva Kendra → Regional Passport Office → Bureau of Immigration → FRRO

scenario: Fake currency, counterfeit money received, received fake notes from ATM or person, forged currency notes, fake coin.
title: Counterfeit Currency
steps: Step 1: Do not use or pass the fake currency to anyone
Step 2: Handle it carefully and preserve it as evidence
Step 3: Report to the nearest police station immediately
Step 4: Submit the fake notes to police — get an acknowledgement receipt
Step 5: Report to your bank if received from ATM — bank will replace it
Step 6: The NIA investigates organized fake currency networks
documents: ATM transaction slip (if received from ATM)
provisions: Section 489A IPC (Counterfeiting currency — up to life imprisonment)
authority: Nearest Police Station → Bank (if from ATM) → NIA (for organized networks)

scenario: Arms act violation, illegal weapon found, unlicensed gun, country-made pistol, illegal firearms, knife attack, carrying prohibited weapon.
title: Illegal Arms / Weapons
steps: Step 1: If falsely implicated, engage a criminal lawyer immediately
Step 2: Demand that the seizure was done following proper procedure with witnesses
Step 3: Challenge the seizure if ARMS Act procedure was not followed
Step 4: Apply for bail immediately in Sessions Court
Step 5: If you found an abandoned weapon, report immediately to police for safe disposal
documents: Arms licence (if you possess a licensed weapon)
provisions: Section 25 Arms Act (Possession of prohibited arms — up to 7 years)
Section 307 IPC (if weapon used for attempt to murder)
authority: Police Station → District Arms Licensing Authority → Sessions Court → High Court

scenario: Riot, communal violence, religious violence, mob lynching, violence during protest, pelting stones on house or shop, curfew violation injuries.
title: Riot / Communal Violence / Mob Lynching
steps: Step 1: Move to a safe location immediately
Step 2: Call police (100) and report the riot or violence
Step 3: File an FIR against identified accused persons
Step 4: Get medical treatment and Medico-Legal Certificate for injuries
Step 5: Apply for riot compensation from the State Government
Step 6: National Human Rights Commission can be approached for serious violations
documents: Photographs of injuries and property damage
provisions: Section 307 IPC (Attempt to murder during riot)
authority: Police (100) → District Magistrate → National Human Rights Commission → State Compensation Authority

scenario: Bank refusing to give loan, loan application rejected without reason, discriminatory loan denial, bank harassing loan applicant, CIBIL score dispute, wrong CIBIL report.
title: Bank Loan Dispute / CIBIL Score Complaint
steps: Step 1: Get your CIBIL report and check for errors at cibil.com
Step 2: If errors found, raise a dispute directly on the CIBIL website
Step 3: Send written complaint to the bank's grievance officer
Step 4: Escalate to the Banking Ombudsman if bank is unresponsive
Step 5: Approach the Consumer Forum for wrongful loan rejection or harassment
Step 6: RBI can be approached if the bank violates fair lending practices
provisions: RBI Fair Practices Code for Lenders
authority: Bank Grievance Officer → Banking Ombudsman → Consumer Forum → RBI

scenario: Will dispute, inheritance fight, property not given after death of parent, brother or sister cheating on property, executor of will not distributing assets, probate of will.
title: Will Dispute / Inheritance / Succession
steps: Step 1: Obtain a copy of the will from the executor or registrar's office
Step 2: Consult a lawyer to understand your legal share under applicable succession law
Step 3: Send a legal notice to the executor or family member misappropriating assets
Step 4: File for probate of the will in the District Court
Step 5: File a partition suit if property is being wrongly withheld
Step 6: Apply for Letters of Administration if there is no will (intestate succession)
provisions: Indian Succession Act 1925 (for Christians and others)
Section 63 Indian Succession Act (Requirements of valid will)
authority: District Court (Probate / Succession Certificate) → Civil Court (Partition Suit) → High Court

scenario: School or college refusing admission, education institution corruption, capitation fee demand, ragging in college, university not giving degree or marksheet, fake university degree.
title: Education Institution Complaint / Ragging
steps: Step 1: For ragging, call the Anti-Ragging Helpline: 1800-180-5522 immediately
Step 2: File a written complaint with the college principal and university
Step 3: Report to UGC / AICTE / respective regulatory body
Step 4: For capitation fee, file complaint with state education department
Step 5: Approach the Consumer Forum for denial of services paid for
Step 6: File a PIL or writ petition in High Court for systemic issues
documents: Medical report (if physically hurt during ragging)
provisions: Right to Education Act 2009 (for school level)
authority: College Principal → University → UGC/AICTE → Anti-Ragging Helpline (1800-180-5522) → Consumer Forum → High Court

scenario: Electricity bill too high, wrong electricity meter reading, power cut complaint, electricity connection refused, electricity department corruption, illegal power disconnection.
title: Electricity / Utility Bill Dispute
steps: Step 1: File a written complaint with your electricity distribution company (DISCOM)
Step 2: Request a meter testing if you believe the reading is wrong
Step 3: Approach the Consumer Grievance Redressal Forum of the electricity company
Step 4: Escalate to the Electricity Ombudsman of your state
Step 5: Approach Consumer Forum if Ombudsman fails to resolve
Step 6: File a writ petition in High Court for illegal disconnection
authority: DISCOM Grievance Cell → Consumer Grievance Redressal Forum → Electricity Ombudsman → Consumer Forum → High Court

scenario: Municipality not providing water supply, garbage not collected, drainage problem, road not repaired, local government negligence, municipality harassment, property tax dispute with municipality.
title: Municipal / Local Government Complaint
steps: Step 1: File a written complaint with your local municipal corporation or panchayat
Step 2: Use the municipality's online grievance portal if available
Step 3: Escalate to the municipal commissioner if no response within 30 days
Step 4: File an RTI to get information about the status of your complaint
Step 5: Approach the Lokpal or Lokayukta for corruption issues
Step 6: File a writ petition in the High Court for violation of civic rights
documents: Photographs of the problem (waterlogging, broken road, etc.)
Aadhaar Card / ID proof with address
provisions: Article 21 of Constitution (Right to clean environment)
Consumer Protection Act 2019 (for paid municipal services)
authority: Municipal Corporation / Panchayat → Municipal Commissioner → Lokayukta → High Court (Writ Petition)

scenario: Environmental pollution complaint, factory causing air or water pollution, chemical waste dumped illegally, noise pollution from industry or construction, river or lake polluted by industry.
title: Environmental Pollution Complaint
steps: Step 1: Document the pollution with photographs, videos, and water/air samples if possible
Step 2: File a complaint with the State Pollution Control Board
Step 3: Report to the National Green Tribunal (NGT) — it has special environmental jurisdiction
Step 4: File an RTI to get pollution test reports from the authority
Step 5: Approach the District Collector for immediate action
Step 6: File a PIL in the High Court for large-scale environmental damage
documents: Photographs and video evidence of pollution
Medical reports if health is affected
Written complaint to Pollution Control Board
provisions: Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act 1974
Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act 1981
authority: State Pollution Control Board → National Green Tribunal (NGT) → District Collector → High Court (PIL)

scenario: Pension not received, government employee pension delayed, pension wrongly stopped, EPF pension dispute, EPFO not releasing provident fund, gratuity not paid by employer.
title: Pension / Gratuity / EPF Dispute
steps: Step 1: File a written complaint with your employer's HR and accounts department
Step 2: For EPF, raise a grievance on the EPFO portal (epfigms.gov.in)
Step 3: For gratuity, file a complaint with the Controlling Authority under Payment of Gratuity Act
Step 4: For government pension, approach the Principal Accounts Office of your department
Step 5: Approach the Labour Court if employer refuses to pay
Step 6: File a consumer complaint if pension service provider is negligent
provisions: Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority Act 2013
Central Civil Services (Pension) Rules 1972 (for govt employees)
authority: EPFO Grievance Portal → Controlling Authority (Gratuity) → Labour Court → Principal Accounts Office (govt pension)

scenario: Disability rights violation, disabled person denied access to public place, wheelchair access refused, disabled employee discriminated, RPWD Act violation, disabled child denied school admission.
title: Disability Rights Violation
steps: Step 1: Document the denial or discrimination with details of the incident
Step 2: File a written complaint with the institution or employer
Step 3: Approach the State Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities
Step 4: File a complaint with the Chief Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities
Step 5: Approach Consumer Forum for denial of services
Step 6: File a writ petition in High Court for violation of constitutional rights
provisions: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act 2016 (RPWD)
Article 21 of Constitution (Right to live with dignity)
UN Convention on Rights of Persons with Disabilities
authority: State Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities → Chief Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities → High Court

scenario: Senior citizen rights violated, old age home abuse, elderly person's property grabbed by children, pension stopped, senior citizen neglected or abandoned by family.
title: Senior Citizen Rights Violation
steps: Step 1: Call the Senior Citizen Helpline: 14567 immediately
Step 2: File a complaint with the local Maintenance Tribunal for Senior Citizens
Step 3: Tribunal can order children to pay monthly maintenance within 90 days
Step 4: If property is being grabbed, file for revocation of property transfer before Tribunal
Step 5: Approach police for protection if there is physical abuse
Step 6: Old age homes are regulated — complain to District Social Welfare Officer for abuse
provisions: Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act 2007
Transfer of Property Act 1882 (for property revocation)
authority: Senior Citizen Helpline (14567) → Maintenance Tribunal → Police Station → District Social Welfare Officer

scenario: Privacy violation, someone sharing my personal data, company sold my personal information, data breach affecting me, Aadhaar data leaked, personal information misused without consent.
title: Privacy Violation / Data Breach
steps: Step 1: Document the privacy violation — what data was shared, where, and by whom
Step 2: Send a legal notice to the company or person who violated your privacy
Step 3: File a complaint with CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team)
Step 4: Report Aadhaar data misuse to UIDAI helpline (1947)
Step 5: Approach Consumer Forum for damages
Step 6: File a writ petition in High Court under Article 21 (Right to Privacy)
documents: Evidence of data breach or privacy violation
Screenshots of where your data appeared
provisions: Article 21 of Constitution (Right to Privacy — K.S. Puttaswamy judgment)
authority: CERT-In → UIDAI (for Aadhaar) → Consumer Forum → High Court (Article 21 Writ)

scenario: Fake news spreading about me, false information published, newspaper or TV channel defaming me, false report in media, journalist spreading lies, media trial causing harm.
title: Defamation / Media Harassment
steps: Step 1: Collect and preserve all defamatory content — articles, videos, screenshots
Step 2: Send a legal notice to the publisher demanding retraction and apology
Step 3: File a criminal defamation complaint under Section 499/500 IPC
Step 4: File a civil defamation suit for damages in District Court
Step 5: Approach the Press Council of India for newspaper defamation
Step 6: Approach News Broadcasting Standards Authority (NBSA) for TV channel defamation
documents: Copies of defamatory articles, videos, or posts
Evidence that the information is false
Proof of harm caused (financial loss, emotional distress)
Legal notice sent and delivery proof
provisions: Section 500 IPC (Punishment for defamation — up to 2 years)
authority: Press Council of India → NBSA (TV channels) → District Court (civil suit) → Police Station (criminal complaint)

scenario: False FIR filed against me, someone falsely accused me of a crime, framed by enemy or rival, malicious prosecution, wrongful arrest, fake case by estranged spouse or relative.
title: False FIR / Malicious Prosecution
steps: Step 1: Engage a criminal lawyer immediately to understand the case against you
Step 2: Apply for anticipatory bail if arrest is likely
Step 3: File for quashing of the FIR in the High Court if it is false and frivolous
Step 4: Gather evidence to prove your innocence — alibi, witnesses, documents
Step 5: After acquittal, file a case for malicious prosecution and damages against the complainant
Step 6: File a counter FIR for false complaint under Section 182/211 IPC
documents: Copy of FIR filed against you
provisions: Section 482 CrPC (High Court power to quash FIR)
Section 182 IPC (False information to public servant)
authority: Criminal Lawyer → Sessions Court (bail) → High Court (quashing FIR) → Civil Court (malicious prosecution damages)

scenario: Contempt of court, court order not being followed, party ignoring court injunction, someone violating court stay order, disobeying judge's directive.
title: Contempt of Court
steps: Step 1: Document the violation of the court order clearly with dates and proof
Step 2: File a contempt petition in the same court that issued the order
Step 3: Attach a copy of the original court order and evidence of violation
Step 4: The court can punish contempt with imprisonment or fine
Step 5: Engage a lawyer to draft the contempt petition properly
documents: Certified copy of the court order being violated
Evidence of violation (photographs, witness statements, documents)
Previous court filings in the case
provisions: Article 129 of Constitution (Supreme Court — contempt power)
Article 215 of Constitution (High Court — contempt power)
Section 10 Contempt of Courts Act (punishment — up to 6 months)
authority: Same Court that issued the order → High Court → Supreme Court

scenario: Business partner cheating, partner running away with company money, partner not sharing profits, illegal dissolution of partnership, partnership deed violated, business fraud by co-founder.
title: Business Partnership Dispute
steps: Step 1: Review your partnership deed to understand rights and obligations
Step 2: Send a legal notice to the erring partner
Step 3: File a civil suit for accounts and dissolution of partnership in Civil Court
Step 4: Apply for injunction to freeze company assets if partner is siphoning funds
Step 5: File an FIR if criminal fraud or misappropriation is involved
Step 6: Approach Company Law Tribunal if it is a registered company (not partnership)
authority: Civil Court → Commercial Court → National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT — for companies) → Police (if criminal fraud)

scenario: Cheating by builder, flat not delivered on time, builder not registering flat, construction quality poor, RERA complaint, builder refusing to refund booking amount, incomplete construction.
title: Builder / Real Estate Fraud (RERA)
steps: Step 1: File a complaint on the state RERA (Real Estate Regulatory Authority) portal
Step 2: Attach the sale agreement, payment receipts, and builder's brochure
Step 3: RERA must resolve complaints within 60 days
Step 4: RERA can order refund with interest or force delivery of flat
Step 5: Also approach Consumer Forum for compensation for mental harassment
Step 6: If fraud is involved, file an FIR against the builder
documents: RERA registration number of the project
provisions: Section 420 IPC (Cheating — if builder commits fraud)
authority: State RERA Authority → Real Estate Appellate Tribunal → Consumer Forum → Police (if fraud)

scenario: Income tax notice, tax department harassment, wrong income tax demand, IT department sending false notice, GST dispute, GST notice, tax raid, black money allegation.
title: Income Tax / GST Dispute
steps: Step 1: Do not ignore the notice — respond within the specified deadline
Step 2: Engage a CA (Chartered Accountant) or tax lawyer immediately
Step 3: File your response with all supporting documents through the income tax portal
Step 4: If the demand is wrong, file a rectification application under Section 154
Step 5: File an appeal before the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals)
Step 6: Approach the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) for second appeal
provisions: Section 246A IT Act (Appeal to CIT Appeals)
authority: Income Tax Department Portal → Commissioner IT (Appeals) → Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) → High Court

scenario: Right to vote denied, name missing from voter list, electoral fraud, candidate threatening voters, booth capturing, EVM tampering allegation, election officer misbehaving.
title: Electoral / Voting Rights Complaint
steps: Step 1: Check your voter registration on voters.eci.gov.in
Step 2: File a complaint with the Returning Officer of your constituency
Step 3: Contact the District Election Officer for voter list corrections
Step 4: Report electoral malpractice to the Election Commission helpline: 1950
Step 5: File an election petition in High Court within 45 days of result for major violations
Step 6: File a complaint with the National Voter Helpline for rights violations
documents: Voter ID card or enrollment number
FIR copy (if criminal activity during election)
authority: Returning Officer → District Election Officer → Election Commission (1950) → High Court (Election Petition)

scenario: Passport not issued, passport application rejected, passport office corruption, tatkaal passport delayed, police verification not done, passport renewal problem.
title: Passport Application / Renewal Issue
steps: Step 1: Check application status on passportindia.gov.in
Step 2: File a grievance on the Passport Seva portal
Step 3: Contact your local Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) with all documents
Step 4: If police verification is pending, contact your local police station
Step 5: File an RTI to know the reason for delay or rejection
Step 6: Approach the Regional Passport Office (RPO) for escalation
provisions: Right to Information Act 2005 (for status queries)
authority: Passport Seva Kendra → Regional Passport Office → Ministry of External Affairs Grievance Portal → High Court (if unreasonably rejected)

scenario: Aadhaar card problem, Aadhaar not linking with bank, Aadhaar biometric failure, Aadhaar update rejected, Aadhaar misused by someone, Aadhaar data stolen.
title: Aadhaar Card Issue / UIDAI Complaint
steps: Step 1: Visit the UIDAI portal (uidai.gov.in) to update or correct Aadhaar details
Step 2: Call UIDAI helpline: 1947 for immediate issues
Step 3: Visit the nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra for biometric updates
Step 4: If Aadhaar is misused, file a complaint with UIDAI and police
Step 5: Lock your Aadhaar biometrics on the UIDAI portal to prevent misuse
Step 6: File RTI with UIDAI if complaints are not resolved
documents: Proof of correct information (birth certificate, address proof)
provisions: Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act 2016
Section 66C IT Act (Identity theft using Aadhaar)
authority: UIDAI Portal (uidai.gov.in) → UIDAI Helpline (1947) → Aadhaar Seva Kendra → Police Station (misuse)

scenario: Neighbours fighting, neighbourhood dispute, noise from neighbours, boundary wall dispute, parking dispute with neighbour, water drainage from neighbours house flooding mine.
title: Neighbourhood / Neighbour Dispute
steps: Step 1: Try to resolve the issue through direct peaceful communication first
Step 2: If noise, file a complaint with local police under Section 133 CrPC
Step 3: For boundary wall or drainage issues, approach the local municipality
Step 4: For parking disputes in housing societies, approach the housing society managing committee
Step 5: Send a legal notice to the neighbour through a lawyer
Step 6: File a civil suit for nuisance or injunction if the problem continues
documents: Photographs or videos of the nuisance
provisions: Section 133 CrPC (Conditional order for nuisance removal)
Transfer of Property Act 1882 (for easement rights)
authority: Local Police Station → Municipal Corporation → Housing Society Managing Committee → Civil Court

scenario: Hospital refusing emergency treatment, patient turned away without treatment, hospital demanding advance payment before treating accident victim, denial of emergency care.
title: Denial of Emergency Medical Treatment
steps: Step 1: Any government hospital MUST provide emergency treatment — they cannot refuse
Step 2: Private hospitals also cannot refuse emergency treatment without first stabilizing the patient
Step 3: Document the denial with date, time, hospital name and names of staff involved
Step 4: File a complaint with the State Medical Council
Step 5: File FIR against the hospital for criminal negligence if patient dies due to refusal
Step 6: Approach Consumer Forum for compensation
documents: Written record of denial (time, hospital, staff details)
Medical bills from the hospital that treated the patient
provisions: Supreme Court order on emergency treatment (Parmanand Katara vs Union of India)
authority: State Medical Council → Consumer Forum → Police Station → High Court (Writ)

scenario: Mental health patient rights violated, forcible detention in psychiatric facility, wrong mental illness diagnosis, mental hospital abuse, involuntary treatment without consent.
title: Mental Health Patient Rights
steps: Step 1: Contact the State Mental Health Authority of your state
Step 2: File a complaint with the Mental Health Review Board
Step 3: Approach the National Human Rights Commission for serious violations
Step 4: File a habeas corpus petition in High Court for wrongful detention
Step 5: Contact a legal aid lawyer for free legal help
documents: Evidence of abuse or wrongful detention
Aadhaar Card / ID proof of patient or guardian
Court order (if any for involuntary admission)
provisions: Article 21 of Constitution (Right to life with dignity)
authority: Mental Health Review Board → State Mental Health Authority → National Human Rights Commission → High Court

scenario: Surrogate mother rights, surrogacy dispute, sperm or egg donor rights, fertility clinic fraud, IVF clinic cheating, reproductive rights violation.
title: Surrogacy / Reproductive Rights Dispute
steps: Step 1: Consult a family lawyer specializing in reproductive law
Step 2: File a complaint with the National Assisted Reproductive Technology Board (if clinic is violating rules)
Step 3: For surrogacy disputes, approach the family court
Step 4: File a consumer complaint if the clinic committed fraud or negligence
Step 5: Approach the State Medical Council against negligent fertility clinic
documents: Aadhaar Card / ID proof of all parties
authority: National ART Board → State ART Board → Family Court → Consumer Forum → State Medical Council

scenario: Forest land encroachment on tribal land, tribal rights violation, scheduled tribe land grabbed, forest rights not given, eviction of tribals from forest land, adivasi rights issue.
title: Tribal / Forest Land Rights
steps: Step 1: File a claim under the Forest Rights Act with the Gram Sabha
Step 2: If claim is rejected, appeal to the Sub-Divisional Level Committee
Step 3: Further appeal to District Level Committee
Step 4: Approach the State Tribal Welfare Department
Step 5: File a writ petition in High Court if rights are being denied
Step 6: National Commission for Scheduled Tribes can be approached
documents: Proof of occupation of forest land for three generations
Land use photographs and witness statements
provisions: Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act 2006
Fifth Schedule of Constitution (protection of tribal areas)
authority: Gram Sabha → Sub-Divisional Level Committee → District Level Committee → State Tribal Welfare Department → High Court

scenario: Cyber crime against company, business email compromise, corporate data theft, hacking of company server, ransomware attack on business, employee stealing company data.
title: Corporate Cyber Crime / Data Theft
steps: Step 1: Immediately isolate the affected systems to contain the breach
Step 2: Report to CERT-In (cert-in.org.in) within 6 hours of discovering the breach
Step 3: File an FIR with the Cyber Crime Cell
Step 4: Engage a digital forensics firm to preserve and analyze evidence
Step 5: Notify affected customers if their data was breached
Step 6: File insurance claim if you have cyber insurance
documents: Evidence of the attack or data theft
List of affected data and systems
provisions: Section 43 IT Act (Penalty for unauthorized access)
Section 66B IT Act (Receiving stolen computer resource)
authority: CERT-In (cert-in.org.in) → Cyber Crime Cell → Police Station → NCIIPC (for critical infrastructure)

scenario: Inheritance of ancestral property, coparcenary rights in Hindu Undivided Family, partition of HUF property, karta of HUF mismanaging property, daughter's rights in ancestral property.
title: Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) / Ancestral Property
steps: Step 1: Consult a civil lawyer to establish your share as a coparcener
Step 2: Send a legal notice to the Karta demanding accounts and partition
Step 3: File a partition suit in the Civil Court
Step 4: A daughter has equal rights in ancestral HUF property (2005 amendment)
Step 5: Approach the Revenue Court for mutation of property in your name after partition
Step 6: Get the partition deed registered after settlement
provisions: Hindu Succession Act 1956 (amended 2005 — daughters' equal rights)
authority: Civil Court (Partition Suit) → Revenue Court (Mutation) → High Court

scenario: Marriage registration problem, wedding not registered, foreign marriage recognition issue, NRI marriage certificate needed, court marriage procedure, interfaith marriage.
title: Marriage Registration / Court Marriage
steps: Step 1: For Hindu marriage, register under Hindu Marriage Act at Sub-Registrar office
Step 2: For court marriage (any religion), apply under Special Marriage Act at Marriage Registrar office with 30 days notice
Step 3: Both parties and three witnesses must be present
Step 4: If marriage registrar is refusing, approach the District Registrar
Step 5: For NRI marriages, register at the nearest Indian Embassy or Consulate abroad
Step 6: Apply for a certified copy of marriage certificate for legal use
documents: Age proof (birth certificate / Aadhaar) of both parties
Aadhaar Card of both parties and 3 witnesses
For Special Marriage Act: 30-day advance notice form
provisions: Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages Act 1886
authority: Sub-Registrar Office (Hindu Marriage) → Marriage Registrar (Special Marriage Act) → District Registrar → Indian Embassy (abroad)

scenario: Child custody dispute, parent not allowing to meet child, one parent took child away, grandparents denied access to grandchild, child abduction by parent, parental rights.
title: Child Custody / Parental Rights
steps: Step 1: File a petition for custody or visitation rights in the Family Court
Step 2: If child has been taken away to another state, file Habeas Corpus petition in High Court
Step 3: The court's primary concern is the welfare of the child
Step 4: Apply for interim custody while the case is pending
Step 5: Mediation is available through Family Courts — consider it for faster resolution
Step 6: For international parental child abduction, contact Ministry of Women and Child Development
documents: Evidence of being a fit parent (financial stability, living conditions)
School and medical records of child
Any previous court orders regarding custody
provisions: Hague Convention on Parental Child Abduction (India not a signatory)
authority: Family Court → High Court (Habeas Corpus for abducted child) → Ministry of Women and Child Development

scenario: Cheating in exam, impersonation in competitive exam, fake degree certificate, question paper leak, exam fraud, UPSC exam malpractice, board exam cheating case.
title: Exam Fraud / Academic Cheating
steps: Step 1: Report to the examination authority immediately with evidence
Step 2: File an FIR at the nearest police station
Step 3: For UPSC or SSC fraud, report directly to the concerned commission
Step 4: For forged degrees, approach the issuing university and UGC
Step 5: Approach the court to cancel fraudulently obtained appointments
Step 6: STF (Special Task Force) usually handles organized exam paper leak cases
provisions: Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act 2024
authority: Police Station → Examination Authority → UGC (fake degrees) → High Court (for appointment cancellation)

scenario: Company not paying its dues to vendor, client refusing to pay invoice, business payment not received, commercial contract breach, non-payment of services rendered to business.
title: Commercial / Business Payment Dispute
steps: Step 1: Send a formal demand notice through a lawyer referencing the contract or invoice
Step 2: File a case in the Commercial Court under the Commercial Courts Act
Step 3: For amounts under Rs 1 crore, approach the Consumer Forum if it's a service
Step 4: Apply for summary judgment under Order 37 CPC for undisputed amounts
Step 5: If company is insolvent, file application under Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code
Step 6: Attach the debtor's assets through court order to secure recovery
documents: Bank statements showing non-receipt of payment
Aadhaar Card / PAN Card of proprietor
provisions: Code of Civil Procedure — Order 37 (Summary Suit)
authority: Commercial Court → MSME Samadhaan (for MSMEs) → Debt Recovery Tribunal → NCLT (insolvency)

scenario: Refugee rights, stateless person, foreign national detained without reason, illegal detention of immigrant, asylum seeker rights, citizenship rights violated.
title: Citizenship / Refugee / Stateless Person Rights
steps: Step 1: Contact the UNHCR (UN Refugee Agency) in India for refugee status determination
Step 2: File a habeas corpus petition in High Court for illegal detention
Step 3: Approach the Ministry of Home Affairs for citizenship or visa issues
Step 4: FRRO (Foreigners Regional Registration Office) handles foreigner registration
Step 5: Contact legal aid organizations like UNHCR or HCR India
provisions: Article 21 of Constitution (applies to non-citizens too)
authority: UNHCR India → FRRO → Ministry of Home Affairs → High Court (Habeas Corpus)

scenario: Flood damage to property, natural disaster loss, compensation for cyclone or earthquake damage, government not giving disaster relief, NDRF not reaching in time.
title: Natural Disaster Relief / Compensation
steps: Step 1: Register your loss with the local tehsildar or revenue officer immediately
Step 2: Apply for relief under the State Disaster Response Fund (SDRF)
Step 3: Keep all evidence — photographs of damage, list of lost items
Step 4: Contact the District Collector's office for immediate relief
Step 5: Approach State Insurance schemes for crop or property loss
Step 6: If relief is denied unfairly, file RTI to check allocation and approach High Court
documents: List of damaged/lost property with estimated value
Bank account details for compensation transfer
authority: Tehsildar / Revenue Officer → District Collector → State Disaster Management Authority → High Court (if relief denied)

scenario: Sports player rights violated, athlete not selected unfairly, doping allegation against player, national sports federation corruption, BCCI or sports body dispute.
title: Sports / Athlete Rights Dispute
steps: Step 1: File a formal complaint with the sports federation's grievance committee
Step 2: Approach the Sports Authority of India (SAI) for national level disputes
Step 3: For doping allegations, appear before the National Anti-Doping Agency (NADA)
Step 4: Approach the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) for international disputes
Step 5: File a writ petition in High Court for fundamental rights violations
documents: Evidence of unfair selection or corruption
provisions: Sports Broadcasting Signals (Mandatory Sharing with Prasar Bharati) Act 2007
authority: Sports Federation Grievance Committee → Sports Authority of India (SAI) → NADA → High Court → Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS)

scenario: Copyright violation, someone copied my work without permission, plagiarism of book or article, music stolen, film script copied, artistic work infringement, design patent stolen.
title: Copyright / Intellectual Property Infringement
steps: Step 1: Document the infringement with screenshots and copies of the stolen work
Step 2: Send a legal cease and desist notice to the infringer
Step 3: File a complaint with the Copyright Office under Ministry of Education
Step 4: File a civil suit for injunction and damages in the District Court
Step 5: For online infringement, send a takedown notice to the platform
Step 6: File a criminal complaint for willful copyright infringement
documents: Original work with creation date proof
authority: Copyright Office → District Court (civil suit) → Intellectual Property Appellate Board → Police (criminal complaint)

scenario: Trademark infringement, competitor copying my brand name or logo, counterfeit products sold under my brand, passing off business name, business identity theft.
title: Trademark / Brand Infringement
steps: Step 1: Apply for or verify trademark registration on IP India portal (ipindia.gov.in)
Step 2: Send a legal cease and desist notice to the infringer immediately
Step 3: File a suit for injunction and damages in the Commercial Court
Step 4: Apply for an ex-parte injunction to immediately stop the infringer
Step 5: File criminal complaint under Section 103 of Trade Marks Act
Step 6: For counterfeit goods, involve Customs authorities
documents: Trademark registration certificate or application number
Evidence of infringement (product samples, photos, advertisements)
Date of first use of the mark
authority: IP India Portal → Commercial Court → Intellectual Property Appellate Board → Customs (for counterfeit imports)

scenario: Stock market fraud, broker cheating, SEBI complaint, mutual fund fraud, Demat account hacked, share market manipulation, IPO fraud, fake investment scheme.
title: Stock Market / Securities Fraud
steps: Step 1: File a complaint on SEBI SCORES portal (scores.sebi.gov.in)
Step 2: Contact your stockbroker's compliance officer and SEBI registered exchange (NSE/BSE)
Step 3: If Demat account is hacked, immediately contact your depository participant
Step 4: File an FIR with the Cyber Crime Cell or Economic Offences Wing
Step 5: For ponzi schemes or fake investment fraud, also report to ED and CBI
Step 6: Approach Consumer Forum for broker negligence
authority: SEBI SCORES Portal → Stock Exchange (NSE/BSE) → EOW Police → Enforcement Directorate → NCLT

scenario: Water dispute between states, river water sharing conflict, irrigation canal blocked, agricultural water rights, well or borewell dispute with neighbour.
title: Water Rights / Irrigation Dispute
steps: Step 1: For agricultural water rights, approach the local Irrigation Department
Step 2: File a complaint with the Water Tribunal if available in your state
Step 3: For inter-state river disputes, the matter goes to the Supreme Court
Step 4: For borewell/well neighbour disputes, approach the Civil Court
Step 5: File RTI to get information on water allocation and distribution
documents: Photographs of blocked canals or water sources
provisions: Article 262 of Constitution (Water disputes between states)
authority: Irrigation Department → Water Tribunal → Civil Court → Supreme Court (inter-state disputes)

scenario: Religious discrimination, denied entry to temple or religious place, religious conversion by force, attacking religious procession, destroying religious property, religious rights violation.
title: Religious Rights Violation / Communal Offence
steps: Step 1: File an FIR immediately at the nearest police station
Step 2: Approach the District Collector/Magistrate for immediate protection
Step 3: Contact National Commission for Minorities for religious minority issues
Step 4: File a complaint with NHRC for serious religious rights violations
Step 5: File a writ petition in High Court for religious freedom violations
documents: Evidence of discrimination or attack (photos, videos)
Religious institution documents if property attacked
provisions: Section 153A IPC (Promoting enmity between religious groups)
Section 295A IPC (Deliberate acts outraging religious feelings)
authority: Police Station → District Magistrate → National Commission for Minorities → NHRC → High Court

scenario: Pollution from neighbour's house, open burning garbage creating smoke, sewage leaking into my property, foul smell from nearby factory or shop, noise pollution from temple or mosque speaker.
title: Private Nuisance / Pollution by Neighbour
steps: Step 1: Document the nuisance with photographs, video, and date-time records
Step 2: File a complaint with the local municipal authority or panchayat
Step 3: File a complaint under Section 133 CrPC for conditional order removing nuisance
Step 4: Send a legal notice to the offending party
Step 5: File a civil suit for damages and injunction in Civil Court
Step 6: For factory pollution, report to State Pollution Control Board
documents: Photographs and videos of the nuisance
Medical records if health is affected
Aadhaar Card / ID proof with address
authority: Municipality → Police (Section 133 CrPC) → Pollution Control Board → Civil Court

scenario: Company director fraud, corporate fraud, managing director looting company, shareholder rights violated, oppression and mismanagement in company, minority shareholder rights.
title: Corporate Fraud / Shareholder Rights
steps: Step 1: File a complaint with the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA portal)
Step 2: Approach the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) for oppression and mismanagement
Step 3: File a complaint with SEBI if it's a listed company
Step 4: Engage a company law advocate for derivative action
Step 5: Report to the Serious Fraud Investigation Office (SFIO) for serious fraud
documents: Share certificates or Demat account statement
Board meeting minutes and financial statements
authority: MCA Portal → NCLT → SEBI (listed companies) → SFIO → High Court

scenario: Tenant not vacating property, tenant refusing to leave after lease expiry, illegal occupation by tenant, rent paid but tenant won't go, squatter on property.
title: Tenant Eviction Dispute
steps: Step 1: Send a formal eviction notice to the tenant as per the rental agreement terms
Step 2: File an eviction suit in the Rent Control Court if tenant refuses to vacate
Step 3: For commercial properties, file a suit for eviction in Civil Court
Step 4: Under the Model Tenancy Act, you can approach the Rent Authority
Step 5: Court can issue a decree for possession, after which bailiff can enforce eviction
Step 6: Do NOT forcibly evict the tenant — this is illegal and can lead to criminal charges against you
documents: Evidence of expiry of lease or violation of terms
Notice served to tenant (with delivery proof)
provisions: Transfer of Property Act 1882 — Section 106 (Notice to quit)
authority: Rent Control Court → Civil Court → District Court → High Court (if eviction decree challenged)

scenario: Bank account frozen or blocked without reason, bank refusing to process transactions, account under PMLA investigation, bank deducting charges without notice, credit card fraud charge back.
title: Bank Account Dispute / Frozen Account
steps: Step 1: Approach your bank branch manager with a written complaint immediately
Step 2: Escalate to the bank's nodal officer if branch doesn't resolve in 30 days
Step 3: File a complaint with the Banking Ombudsman of your region
Step 4: For PMLA freeze, engage a lawyer to appear before the ED and challenge the freeze
Step 5: Approach Consumer Forum for unauthorized deductions
Step 6: File a writ petition in High Court if constitutional rights are violated
documents: Written complaint to bank with acknowledgement
Evidence of unauthorized deduction or freeze notice
provisions: Prevention of Money Laundering Act 2002 (for PMLA freeze)
authority: Bank Branch → Bank Nodal Officer → Banking Ombudsman → Consumer Forum → High Court (writ)

scenario: Right to food denied, ration card problem, PDS shop not giving ration, ration card cancelled without reason, BPL status wrongly removed, food security entitlement denied.
title: Food Security / Ration Card Dispute
steps: Step 1: File a complaint with your local food supply officer or tehsildar
Step 2: Approach the District Supply Officer for ration card correction or reinstatement
Step 3: File an RTI to know why your ration was stopped or card cancelled
Step 4: Approach the State Food Commission under NFSA
Step 5: File a writ petition in High Court — right to food is a fundamental right
documents: Ration card (or rejection letter if new application)
provisions: Article 21 of Constitution (Right to food as right to life)
authority: Local Food Supply Officer → District Supply Officer → State Food Commission → High Court

scenario: Scholarship not given to SC ST OBC student, reservation benefits denied, caste certificate rejected, OBC creamy layer wrongly classified, government job reservation violated.
title: Reservation / Scholarship Benefit Denial
steps: Step 1: File a written complaint with the concerned educational institution or government department
Step 2: Approach the Social Welfare Department of your state
Step 3: File a complaint with National Commission for SC/ST/OBC as applicable
Step 4: File an RTI to understand why the benefit was denied
Step 5: File a writ petition in High Court for violation of constitutional reservation rights
provisions: Article 16 of Constitution (Reservation in public employment)
Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes Orders
authority: Social Welfare Department → National Commission for SC/ST/OBC → High Court (Writ)

scenario: Land acquisition by government without fair compensation, forcible land acquisition, rehabilitation not given after land taken by government, smart city project displacing residents.
title: Government Land Acquisition Dispute
steps: Step 1: Check if proper procedure under LARR Act 2013 was followed
Step 2: File objections before the Land Acquisition Collector within 60 days of notification
Step 3: Appear at the public hearing mandatory under LARR Act
Step 4: If compensation is inadequate, file a reference before the Land Acquisition Court
Step 5: Challenge arbitrary acquisition in the High Court via writ petition
Step 6: Approach the Rehabilitation and Resettlement Authority for R&R benefits
documents: Original land ownership documents (patta, title deed)
provisions: Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act 2013 (LARR Act)
authority: Land Acquisition Collector → Land Acquisition Court → Rehabilitation Authority → High Court

scenario: Online shopping fraud, product not delivered, fake e-commerce website, payment made but order cancelled, e-commerce seller cheating, Amazon Flipkart Meesho refund problem.
title: E-Commerce / Online Shopping Fraud
steps: Step 1: First exhaust the platform's own return/refund/escalation process
Step 2: File a complaint on consumerhelpline.gov.in — e-commerce is covered
Step 3: File a complaint with the National Consumer Helpline (1800-11-4000)
Step 4: Approach Consumer Forum — District Commission for amounts up to Rs 1 crore
Step 5: For fake websites, file on cybercrime.gov.in and report to cyber cell
Step 6: File complaint with payment gateway (Razorpay, Paytm etc.) for chargeback
documents: Screenshots of the product listing and description
authority: E-Commerce Platform Grievance Officer → National Consumer Helpline (1800-11-4000) → District Consumer Commission → Cyber Cell

scenario: Corruption by government officer, bribe demanded by government employee, public servant demanding money for work, government office not doing work without bribe, corrupt official.
title: Government Corruption / Bribery
steps: Step 1: Report to the Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) or Lokayukta of your state
Step 2: You can also lay a trap with ACB assistance to catch the corrupt official red-handed
Step 3: File a complaint with the CBI for central government officials
Step 4: File a complaint on the CVC (Central Vigilance Commission) portal for central govt
Step 5: File RTI to put pressure on officials
Step 6: Approach the High Court if no action is taken
documents: Evidence of bribe demand (recording if possible)
Details of the work being delayed and the official involved
provisions: Section 7 PC Act (Public servant taking bribe)
authority: Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) → Lokayukta → CBI (central govt) → CVC → High Court

scenario: Tenant being illegally evicted, landlord throwing out tenant without court order, landlord cutting electricity and water supply to force tenant out, forcible eviction.
title: Illegal Eviction / Landlord Harassment of Tenant
steps: Step 1: Call police (100) if landlord is forcibly evicting without court order
Step 2: File an FIR against the landlord for illegal eviction
Step 3: File a complaint in the Rent Control Court for protection
Step 4: Apply for restoration of electricity and water supply in Magistrate court
Step 5: File a suit for damages for illegal eviction
documents: Evidence of illegal eviction (photos, videos, witnesses)
Written communication from landlord threatening eviction
authority: Police Station (100) → Rent Control Court → Magistrate Court → Civil Court

scenario: Minor married off by parents, child marriage, forced marriage of girl below 18 or boy below 21, parents arranging marriage of underage child.
title: Child Marriage
steps: Step 1: Call Childline helpline: 1098 immediately
Step 2: File a complaint with the Child Marriage Prohibition Officer (every district has one)
Step 3: File an FIR at the nearest police station
Step 4: Approach the Child Welfare Committee for protection of the child
Step 5: Court can declare child marriage void and prosecute parents and organizers
documents: Evidence of child marriage being arranged or conducted
Age proof of the child (birth certificate, school records)
Photographs or witnesses to the marriage
provisions: Section 9 PCMA (Punishment for male adult marrying a child)
Section 10 PCMA (Punishment for solemnizing child marriage)
POCSO Act 2012 (if sexual abuse is involved)
IPC Section 376 (if rape within child marriage)
authority: Childline (1098) → Police Station → Child Marriage Prohibition Officer → Child Welfare Committee

scenario: Honour killing threat, family threatening to kill for marrying outside caste or religion, inter-caste couple being harassed, khap panchayat interference, love marriage violence.
title: Honour Killing / Inter-Caste Marriage Threat
steps: Step 1: Contact police immediately for protection
Step 2: File a complaint in the High Court for police protection — courts take this very seriously
Step 3: Apply for witness protection
Step 4: Approach Special Marriage Act court for registering marriage legally
Step 5: NGOs like Shakti Vahini provide shelter and legal help for such couples
documents: Marriage certificate or proof of relationship
provisions: Special Marriage Act 1954 (for legal protection of inter-caste/religion marriages)
SC Guidelines in Shakti Vahini vs Union of India (2018)
authority: Police Station → High Court (urgent protection) → NGO Support (Shakti Vahini) → Special Marriage Registrar

scenario: LGBTQ rights violated, discrimination based on sexual orientation, transgender person harassed, trans person denied employment or housing, Section 377 misuse, gender identity rights.
title: LGBTQ+ / Transgender Rights
steps: Step 1: File a written complaint with the employer or institution that discriminated
Step 2: Approach the National Commission for Women or NHRC
Step 3: File a complaint with the police under Transgender Persons Act if harassed
Step 4: For employment discrimination, approach the Labour Commissioner
Step 5: File a writ petition in High Court for constitutional rights violations
provisions: Section 18 Transgender Act (Penalties for offences against transgender persons)
Navtej Singh Johar vs Union of India 2018 (Section 377 decriminalized)
authority: Police Station → Labour Commissioner (employment) → NHRC → High Court (Writ)

scenario: Journalist arrested for reporting, press freedom violation, media censorship, reporter harassed by government, blogger arrested for articles, social media post leading to arrest.
title: Press Freedom / Free Speech Violation
steps: Step 1: Contact the Press Club of India or Press Freedom organizations immediately
Step 2: Engage a lawyer and file for bail immediately
Step 3: File a petition in High Court for bail and challenging the arrest
Step 4: Approach the Press Council of India for media freedom cases
Step 5: Document and report the incident to international press freedom organizations
documents: The article or content that led to arrest
provisions: Article 19(1)(a) of Constitution (Freedom of speech and expression)
IT Act Section 66A (struck down but misused)
Section 505 IPC (Statements conducing to public mischief)
Romesh Thappar vs State of Madras (press freedom)
authority: Criminal Lawyer → High Court (Bail + Quashing) → Supreme Court → Press Council of India → NHRC

scenario: Internet shutdown by government, website blocked without reason, VPN blocked, access to information denied, government blocking websites arbitrarily, online censorship.
title: Internet Shutdown / Website Blocking
steps: Step 1: Document the shutdown or blocking with screenshots and timestamps
Step 2: File an RTI with the Ministry of Electronics and IT about the order
Step 3: File a writ petition in High Court challenging the shutdown
Step 4: Contact digital rights organizations like Internet Freedom Foundation
Step 5: Supreme Court has held internet access is a right — cite Anuradha Bhasin judgment
documents: Evidence of internet shutdown or website block
Proof of harm caused by the shutdown
provisions: Temporary Suspension of Telecom Services Rules 2017
Anuradha Bhasin vs Union of India 2020 (SC — internet as right)
authority: RTI Officer (DoT) → High Court (Writ) → Supreme Court

scenario: Bonded labour in brick kiln, farmer debt bondage, labourer not allowed to leave employer, migrant worker exploited, factory worker rights violated, unpaid migrant worker.
title: Bonded Labour / Worker Exploitation
steps: Step 1: Contact the District Magistrate for immediate rescue — DM has power to free bonded labourers
Step 2: File a complaint with the Labour Department
Step 3: Contact Anti-Human Trafficking Unit or police
Step 4: Apply for rehabilitation under the Central Sector Scheme for Rehabilitation
Step 5: File FIR against employer for illegal confinement and forced labour
documents: Any bondage agreement (null and void under law)
Evidence of forced labour or debt bondage
Medical records if workers were harmed
authority: District Magistrate → Labour Department → Anti-Human Trafficking Unit → Police Station

scenario: Adoption problem, adoption agency fraud, illegal adoption, child given for adoption without proper procedure, NRI adoption dispute, adoption certificate not given.
title: Adoption Dispute
steps: Step 1: All legal adoptions must go through CARA (Central Adoption Resource Authority)
Step 2: File a complaint with CARA if adoption agency is committing fraud
Step 3: Approach the Juvenile Justice Board for illegal adoptions
Step 4: File a petition in the Family Court for adoption-related disputes
Step 5: For NRI adoptions, CARA coordinates with the receiving country's authority
documents: Adoption application and CARA registration documents
Child's birth certificate and background report
Aadhaar Card / ID proof of adoptive parents
provisions: Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act 2015
Hindu Adoptions and Maintenance Act 1956 (for Hindus)
authority: CARA (cara.nic.in) → Juvenile Justice Board → Family Court → High Court

scenario: Pension fund fraud, insurance agent fraud, chit fund fraud, Ponzi scheme, pyramid scheme, multi-level marketing fraud, PACL type investment fraud, deposits not returned.
title: Investment / Chit Fund / Ponzi Fraud
steps: Step 1: File an FIR at the nearest police station
Step 2: File a complaint with SEBI if it's a securities-related scheme (scores.sebi.gov.in)
Step 3: Report to the Enforcement Directorate (ED) for money laundering aspects
Step 4: Report to the state police Economic Offences Wing (EOW)
Step 5: File a complaint with RBI if it involves deposit-taking by an unregistered NBFC
Step 6: Join other victims — class action gives more weight to the complaint
documents: Agreement with the chit fund or scheme
provisions: Prize Chits and Money Circulation Schemes (Banning) Act 1978
SEBI Act 1992 (for illegal collective investment schemes)
