Banning of Unregulated Deposit Schemes Act 2019
authority: Police EOW → SEBI → RBI → Enforcement Directorate → SFIO → State CID

scenario: Gold or jewellery fraud, jeweller gave less gold, under-karatage gold, fake hallmark on jewellery, BIS hallmark fraud, sold impure gold, jewellery not as described.
title: Gold / Jewellery Fraud
steps: Step 1: Get the jewellery independently tested at a BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards) certified hallmarking centre
Step 2: File a complaint with the BIS (bis.gov.in) if hallmark fraud is confirmed
Step 3: File a complaint with the Consumer Forum
Step 4: File an FIR for cheating under Section 420 IPC
Step 5: Contact the jewellers' association or trade body
provisions: Legal Metrology Act 2009 (for weights and measures)
authority: BIS (bis.gov.in) → Consumer Forum → Police Station (FIR) → Legal Metrology Department

scenario: Petrol pump fraud, tampered meter at fuel station, short fuel given, adulterated fuel, fuel quality complaint, wrong price charged at petrol pump.
title: Petrol Pump / Fuel Fraud
steps: Step 1: Note the reading before and after fuelling as evidence
Step 2: Demand to see the sealing certificate of the fuel dispenser
Step 3: File a complaint with the oil marketing company (BPCL/IOCL/HPCL) helpline
Step 4: File a complaint with the Legal Metrology Department of your district
Step 5: Approach Consumer Forum for compensation
documents: Photo or video of the meter reading
authority: Oil Company Helpline → Legal Metrology Department → Consumer Forum → Police (if criminal fraud)

scenario: Hospital billing fraud, overcharged by hospital, medical bill inflated, insurance claim inflated by hospital, unnecessary treatment done to increase bill, ghost patient fraud.
title: Hospital Billing Fraud / Overcharging
steps: Step 1: Demand itemized bill from hospital — they are legally required to provide it
Step 2: File a complaint with hospital's patient grievance cell
Step 3: Report to your insurance company if insurance fraud is involved
Step 4: Approach the Consumer Forum for overcharging
Step 5: File complaint with State Medical Council against the hospital
Step 6: For government hospital overcharging, approach the Health Department
documents: Evidence of specific overcharging or unnecessary treatment
provisions: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority regulations
State Health Department guidelines on hospital rates
authority: Hospital Grievance Cell → Insurance Ombudsman (if insurance involved) → Consumer Forum → State Medical Council → Health Department

scenario: Fake product sold, counterfeit goods, spurious medicines, fake branded goods, adulterated food, food poisoning from restaurant, substandard drugs sold.
title: Counterfeit Products / Food Adulteration
steps: Step 1: Preserve the product as evidence — do not discard it
Step 2: For medicines, report to the State Drug Controller immediately
Step 3: For food items, report to the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI)
Step 4: File an FIR for selling counterfeit or adulterated products
Step 5: File a consumer complaint for compensation
documents: Medical report if health was affected
authority: FSSAI → State Drug Controller → Consumer Forum → Police Station (FIR)

scenario: Mobile tower radiation complaint, telecom tower installed illegally on building, tower near school or hospital, radiation health concerns, illegal telecom tower.
title: Telecom Tower / EMF Radiation Complaint
steps: Step 1: Check if the tower has valid permissions from municipal authority
Step 2: File a complaint with the local municipal corporation
Step 3: File a complaint with the TRAI (Telecom Regulatory Authority of India)
Step 4: Approach the DoT (Department of Telecommunications) for safety norms compliance
Step 5: File a writ in High Court if health concerns are serious
documents: Photographs of the tower and its location
Evidence it is near school, hospital, or residential area
Any health reports linked to radiation
provisions: Telecom Engineering Centre (TEC) EMF Radiation Norms
authority: Municipal Corporation → TRAI → Department of Telecommunications → High Court (if urgent)

scenario: Private school overcharging fees, school forcing to buy books and stationery from school only, school collecting donation illegally, fee hike without approval, refund of fees after withdrawal.
title: School Fee / Education Overcharging
steps: Step 1: File a complaint with the school's management committee
Step 2: Approach the District Education Officer (DEO)
Step 3: File a complaint with the Fee Regulatory Committee if your state has one
Step 4: File a consumer complaint — education is a service under Consumer Protection Act
Step 5: File a complaint with the state Private School Regulatory Authority
documents: Fee receipts and demand letters from school
Aadhaar Card / ID proof of parent
provisions: State Private School Fee Regulation Acts
Supreme Court Guidelines on school fees
authority: School Management → District Education Officer → Fee Regulatory Committee → Consumer Forum → High Court

scenario: Ambulance not reaching in time, 108 ambulance delay, patient died due to ambulance delay, hospital ambulance overcharging, private ambulance fraud.
title: Ambulance / Emergency Service Failure
steps: Step 1: Document the time of call and time of arrival for evidence
Step 2: File a complaint with the 108 ambulance service authority of your state
Step 3: Report to the State Health Department
Step 4: File a consumer complaint for overcharging by private ambulance
Step 5: File FIR if negligent delay led to death
documents: Call records to 108 or ambulance service
authority: 108 Ambulance Authority → State Health Department → Consumer Forum → Police Station (if negligence led to death)

scenario: Notary fraud, fake notarized document, forged stamp paper, stamp paper fraud, document registration fraud, sub-registrar corruption, fake sale deed registered.
title: Document Forgery / Registration Fraud
steps: Step 1: File an FIR at the police station immediately with the forged document as evidence
Step 2: File a complaint with the Sub-Registrar's office and Inspector General of Registration
Step 3: File an application to cancel the fraudulent registration in the civil court
Step 4: File a complaint with the State Notary Authority (for notary fraud)
Step 5: Approach Revenue Court to correct fraudulent entries in land records
documents: Original genuine documents to compare with forgery
Witnesses who can identify the fraud
authority: Police Station → Sub-Registrar Office → Inspector General of Registration → Civil Court → Revenue Court

scenario: Car or bike insurance not giving full value for accident claim, cashless insurance rejected, motor insurance claim delayed, surveyor giving low value for damaged vehicle, insurance total loss dispute.
title: Motor Vehicle Insurance Claim Dispute
steps: Step 1: Get an independent vehicle valuation report to counter the surveyor's estimate
Step 2: Send written objection to the insurance company's assessment
Step 3: Escalate to Insurance Company's Grievance Officer
Step 4: Approach the Insurance Ombudsman of your region
Step 5: File in Consumer Forum — motor insurance disputes are very commonly resolved here
Step 6: Approach MACT (Motor Accident Claims Tribunal) if third party is involved
authority: Insurance Company → Insurance Ombudsman → Consumer Forum → MACT (for third party)
